1.question: How accurate is the MBTI test?
1.answer: MBTI is a personality type analysis tool based on statistical probabilities. It is not an absolute diagnostic tool, and behavior may vary depending on the situation. Please use it for reference purposes.

2.question: How long does the test take?
2.answer: The test consists of 40 questions and takes approximately 10-15 minutes to complete. Take your time and answer honestly.

3.question: How do I interpret the results?
3.answer: On the results page, you can see your detailed MBTI type description, strengths, weaknesses, and suitable career paths.

4.question: Is the test free?
4.answer: Yes, it is completely free. You can start the test right away without any registration or payment.

mbtiStory.title: Your Journey to Discovering Personality
mbtiStory.subtitle: A concise MBTI guide based on validated psychological theories
mbtiStory.p1: The MBTI (Myers-Briggs Type Indicator) classifies personalities into 16 types based on Carl Jung's theory of psychological types. Beyond a simple psychological test, it helps you deeply understand how you communicate with the world and process energy.
mbtiStory.p2: The questions on our website are carefully designed based on multi-faceted data derived from continuous psychological research validation. Discover hints covering a wide range of areas, including career compatibility, relationship strengths and weaknesses, dating compatibility, and stress management.
mbtiStory.p3: Answer 40 carefully selected questions right now, and uncover the amazing potential and true self hidden within you.
mbtiStory.authorTip: Psychology Editor's Tip: For accurate results, we recommend answering based on your natural self rather than thinking too long.

insights.title: Daily MBTI Insights
insights.subtitle: Useful personality psychology knowledge to know
insights.fact1: The rarest personality type is INFJ, making up about 1-2% of the global population. They possess deep insight and strong intuition.
insights.fact2: The difference between E (Extraversion) and I (Introversion) is not merely sociability but how one gains energy. Extraverts recharge from external stimuli, while introverts recharge from inner rest.
insights.fact3: MBTI results are not fixed! As you age, change careers, or experience different environments, the functions you consciously use may change, altering your results.

compatibility.title: MBTI Compatibility
compatibility.subtitle: Check compatibility between types and learn relationship tips
compatibility.defaultDescription: Your different personalities create a balanced relationship.
compatibility.defaultAdvice: Respect your differences and learn from each other as you grow together.

level90.title: 90-100 points
level90.name: Soulmates
level90.desc: Personalities are very well matched. An ideal relationship where you understand and complement each other.

level80.title: 80-89 points
level80.name: Great Match
level80.desc: You get along well and can grow together.

level70.title: 70-79 points
level70.name: Good Relationship
level70.desc: There are differences, but with effort, you can maintain a good relationship.

levelBelow70.title: Below 70 points
levelBelow70.name: Needs Effort
levelBelow70.desc: There are many differences. Understanding and respecting each other is important.

tips: Understand how your partner expresses love (words vs actions)
When conflicts arise, acknowledge differences and find compromise
Develop common interests and find activities you can enjoy together
Consider each other's strengths when dividing household roles
Adapt to differences in communication styles
Regularly share your feelings and thoughts with each other
Choose education methods considering your child's MBTI
Support their growth through encouragement rather than pressure
Find learning styles that match your child's personality type

intjEnfp.title: Architect × Campaigner
intjEnfp.desc: Perfect combination of strategic thinking (INTJ) and creative energy (ENFP). Maximizes each other's strengths.

istjEsfp.title: Logistician × Entertainer
istjEsfp.desc: Balance of stability (ISTJ) and energy (ESFP). Complements each other's weaknesses.

infjEnfp.title: Advocate × Campaigner
infjEnfp.desc: Harmony of deep empathy and idealism. Understands and supports each other's emotions well.

faq.title: Frequently Asked Questions
faq.question1: The results are a bit different from what I usually think.
faq.answer1: MBTI can be influenced by your psychological state or environment at the time. We recommend trying again while thinking of your most comfortable self, or exploring the reasons deeply through the 'Recommended Columns' at the bottom of the report.
faq.question2: Can I record my test history separately?
faq.answer2: Currently, results are stored temporarily in your browser. We are preparing a membership feature so you can create your own 'Psychology Archive'. Please stay tuned!
faq.question3: How is this different from professional psychological tests?
faq.answer3: Our service is an 'Analysis Guide' that blends latest psychological data with popular MBTI theories for accessibility. While we maintain academic rigor, please note there may be differences from clinical hospital tests.
faq.question4: Can I cite data for business or academic purposes?
faq.answer4: You are more than welcome to cite us as long as you clearly state the source (Simple MBTI). For large-scale citations or collaborations, please leave a message via the 'Inspiration & Proposals' channel.

privacyPolicy.title: Privacy Policy
privacyPolicy.subtitle: Simple MBTI's policy on personal information processing

article1.title: Article 1 (Purpose)
article1.content: Simple MBTI (hereinafter referred to as the "Service") values users' personal information and complies with relevant laws such as the 「Personal Information Protection Act」. This Privacy Policy informs you of how and for what purposes your personal information is used, and what measures are taken to protect your personal information.

article2.title: Article 2 (Personal Information Collected)
article2.content: The Service collects the following personal information for membership registration and service usage.

article3.title: Article 3 (Purpose of Personal Information Collection and Use)
article3.content: The collected personal information is used for the following purposes.
items: MBTI test result storage and retrieval

article4.title: Article 4 (Retention and Use Period of Personal Information)
article4.content: Users' personal information is, in principle, promptly deleted upon achievement of the purpose of collection and use. However, if retention is required under relevant regulations, it will be retained for the period specified by law.

article5.title: Article 5 (Procedures and Methods of Personal Information Destruction)
article5.content: The Service, in principle, promptly deletes information once the purpose of personal information collection and use has been achieved.
items: Personal information stored in electronic files is deleted using technical methods that make reproduction impossible
Personal information recorded on paper documents is destroyed by shredding or incineration

article6.title: Article 6 (User Rights and How to Exercise Them)
article6.content: Users can view, modify, or request deletion of their registered personal information at any time.
items: View/Modify personal information: Profile in settings menu
Delete personal information: Contact customer center
Withdraw consent: Personal information in settings menu

article7.title: Article 7 (Measures to Ensure Safety of Personal Information)
article7.content: The Service takes the following measures to safely manage users' personal information.
items: Encryption: Personal information is encrypted for storage and transmission
Access restriction: Minimize access to personal information
Security programs: Install and operate latest security programs
Regular audits: Regularly inspect personal information handling status

article8.title: Article 8 (Changes to Privacy Policy)
article8.content: This Privacy Policy may be changed according to laws and policies, and when changes are made, users will be notified at least 7 days in advance through announcements on the service.

article9.title: Article 9 (Advertising and Third-Party Cookies)
article9.content: This service displays advertisements through third-party advertising networks including Google AdSense. For this purpose, Google and third-party advertising partners store cookies on users' browsers to provide personalized ads tailored to users' interests.

about.metadataTitle: About - Simple MBTI | Understanding Humanity Through Data
about.metadataDescription: Discover the philosophy and research methodology of Simple MBTI. We go beyond simple tests to provide deep behavioral analysis for better self-understanding.
about.ogDescription: Where psychological insight meets data science. Discover the mission of Simple MBTI.
about.heroSubtitle: Beyond simple categorization, we discover your unique patterns through data and psychology.
about.missionDescription: Simple MBTI aims to be a flexible mirror reflecting the world, rather than a rigid frame defining 'me'. We combine validated psychological theories with vast behavioral data to design touchpoints where users can discover their potential and empathize more deeply with others. We approach the realm of emotions—things numbers cannot tell—in the most scientific way possible.
about.scienceDesc: Reliable analysis based on extensive statistics and empirical research.
about.expertiseDesc: Meticulous supervision by active psychologists and data scientists.
about.practicalityDesc: Practical guides applicable to real life, going beyond simple results.
about.expert1Title: Clinical Psychology Analyst / Behavioral Data Specialist
about.expert1Edu: Masters/Ph.D. Candidate in Psychology, Seoul National University
about.expert1Work: Former Researcher at Corporate Insight Strategy Team
about.expert1Member: Regular Member, Cognitive Analysis Division, Korean Psychological Association
about.expert1Exp: 12+ years in digital psychometric tool design
about.expert1Quote: We hope our tool serves as a compass to navigate the wider world, rather than a partition that limits you.
about.expert2Edu: MA in Counseling Psychology, Yonsei University
about.expert2Cert: Level 1 Youth Counselor & Certified Career Counselor
about.expert2Exp: Over 1,500 sessions of executive coaching and group counseling
about.expert2Quote: Every personality has its own unique beauty. Our job is to read and understand those patterns.
about.researchBaseDesc: All our reports and analysis are built upon authoritative psychological bibles and the latest research papers:
about.standardizedQuestionsDesc: Question design that minimizes social desirability bias.
about.balancedPlacementDesc: Precision scoring considering correlations between items, not just simple summation.
about.scoreWeightDesc: Statistical corrections reflecting latest response trends.
about.privacyDesc: Complete anonymization and highest level of security solutions.
about.partnershipsDesc: We collaborate closely with domestic and international psychological and data research institutes to maintain academic rigor.
about.limitation1: MBTI is a tool for understanding preferences, not a diagnosis that limits your potential.
about.limitation2: Human psychology is multi-dimensional; please recognize that the results are only a part of you.
about.limitation3: We urge you not to misuse the results as a tool to criticize or discriminate against others.
about.limitation4: Your data is encrypted and managed solely for service improvement and analysis.
about.limitation5: If you are experiencing severe psychological distress, we strongly recommend professional consultation.
about.contactDesc: We welcome anything from feedback on analysis results to partnership proposals or a warm hello.
about.editorialDesc: Before a column reaches the world, it undergoes three stages of verification.
about.step1Desc: Establish timely topics by analyzing latest psychology trends and user data.
about.step2Desc: Create readable yet in-depth content by blending theoretical backgrounds with actual behavioral cases.
about.step3Desc: Professional editors and psychologists perform final reviews for accuracy and ethical suitability.
about.step4Desc: We constantly supplement existing content when new research findings emerge, maintaining its vitality.

usage.title: Practical Applications
usage.subtitle: Learn how to effectively use MBTI in your daily life, career, and relationships
usage.stressManagementDesc: Learn effective stress relief methods for each personality type
usage.timeManagementDesc: Apply time management methods suited to your type
usage.decisionMakingDesc: Use decision-making methods that leverage each type's strengths
usage.understandLoveLanguage: Understand how your partner expresses love (words vs actions)
usage.acceptDifferences: Accept differences and find compromise during conflicts
usage.commonInterests: Develop common interests and find activities to enjoy together
usage.roleDistribution: Consider each other's strengths when dividing household roles
usage.adaptCommunication: Adapt to differences in communication styles
usage.regularSharing: Regularly share feelings and thoughts with each other
usage.mbtiBasedEducation: Choose education methods based on your child's MBTI
usage.encouragement: Support growth through encouragement rather than coercion
usage.learningStyle: Find learning styles that match your child's personality type
usage.warningContent: MBTI is just a reference tool and does not perfectly describe your personality. Behavior can vary depending on the situation. If you need professional counseling, please seek help from a qualified expert.

glossary.title: Glossary
glossary.subtitle: Easily understand MBTI terms and concepts

beginner.title: For Beginners
tips: Start with the basic concepts of the four dichotomies
Accurately identify your own type
Read the type characteristics repeatedly
Analyze the types of people close to you
Don't treat MBTI results as absolute truth

advanced.title: Advanced Study
tips: Study the concept of cognitive functions in depth
Understand the function stack of each type
Observe behavior patterns under stress
Interact with people of various types
Learn about MBTI limitations and misconceptions

practical.title: Practical Application
tips: Consider the other person's type when communicating
Apply type-specific coping methods in conflicts
Utilize your strengths in career choices
Respect each other's differences in relationships
Practice continuous self-reflection

warning.title: Cautions
tips: MBTI is not a scientific diagnostic tool
Don't categorize people simply as labels
Types can change with growth and circumstances
It cannot replace professional counseling
Continuous learning and flexible thinking are important

usage.title: Practical Usage
usage.description: Career, dating, and daily life applications

author.title: About the Author
author.name: Dr. Junyoung Park
author.bio: Dr. Park holds a Ph.D. in Clinical Psychology and has analyzed behavioral patterns and psychological data for over 15 years. Based on over 100,000 cases of psychological test data, he provides scientific, highly actionable insights for daily life—from career and dating to personal development.
